title: What Lies in the Woods
author: Kate Alice Marshall
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "What Lies in the Woods" book by Kate Alice Marshall?
Options:
A. Big Jim's name matched his truly big physique. Oscar got his huge frame and blocky features from him. Jim was a looming figure, both literally and metaphorically, in Chester. He'd been the mayor for twenty-eight years, with his only real opponent being Clark Jensen, a war veteran who'd saved comrades but still lost the election by six points. I was never sure where I stood with Jim. He didn't seem to dislike me, but he didn't seem to like me either. Whenever we talked while I was growing up, he acted as if he hadn't even noticed I was there. 
B. Big Jim was aptly named for his large stature. He was the source of Oscar's massive build and square face. He held an imposing presence both literally and figuratively in Chester. He had been the mayor for twenty-eight years, with the only challenger being war hero Clark Jensen, who still lost by six points despite his valor. I was never certain if Jim liked me or not. He did not seem to harbor any dislike, but also did not pay me much attention. Whenever we interacted while I was young, he acted surprised to see me there.
C. Big Jim's moniker was well-earned based on his large stature. He was the source of Oscar's massive build and square facial structure. He held an imposing presence, both physically and influentially, in Chester. He had served as mayor for twenty-eight years, with his sole challenger being Clark Jensen, a war hero who rescued fellow soldiers yet still lost the election by six points. I was never certain if Jim liked me. He did not appear to harbor any ill will, but also did not pay me much attention. Whenever we interacted during my youth, he seemed surprised to see me there.
D. Big Jim came by his name honestly. He was where Oscar had gotten his massive frame and squared-off features. He loomed literally as well as figuratively in Chester. Hed been mayor for twenty-eight years, and the only person whod ever come close to unseating him was Clark Jensen, whod carried three wounded fellow soldiers through a hail of gunfire and still lost the election by six points. Id never been sure where I stood with Jim. He didnt like me, but he didnt dislike me either, as far as I could tell. Every time Id talked to him growing up, hed seemed surprised, like he hadnt noticed I was there.
Answer:

options.A: Big Jim's name matched his truly big physique. Oscar got his huge frame and blocky features from him. Jim was a looming figure, both literally and metaphorically, in Chester. He'd been the mayor for twenty-eight years, with his only real opponent being Clark Jensen, a war veteran who'd saved comrades but still lost the election by six points. I was never sure where I stood with Jim. He didn't seem to dislike me, but he didn't seem to like me either. Whenever we talked while I was growing up, he acted as if he hadn't even noticed I was there.
options.B: Big Jim was aptly named for his large stature. He was the source of Oscar's massive build and square face. He held an imposing presence both literally and figuratively in Chester. He had been the mayor for twenty-eight years, with the only challenger being war hero Clark Jensen, who still lost by six points despite his valor. I was never certain if Jim liked me or not. He did not seem to harbor any dislike, but also did not pay me much attention. Whenever we interacted while I was young, he acted surprised to see me there.
options.C: Big Jim's moniker was well-earned based on his large stature. He was the source of Oscar's massive build and square facial structure. He held an imposing presence, both physically and influentially, in Chester. He had served as mayor for twenty-eight years, with his sole challenger being Clark Jensen, a war hero who rescued fellow soldiers yet still lost the election by six points. I was never certain if Jim liked me. He did not appear to harbor any ill will, but also did not pay me much attention. Whenever we interacted during my youth, he seemed surprised to see me there.
options.D: Big Jim came by his name honestly. He was where Oscar had gotten his massive frame and squared-off features. He loomed literally as well as figuratively in Chester. Hed been mayor for twenty-eight years, and the only person whod ever come close to unseating him was Clark Jensen, whod carried three wounded fellow soldiers through a hail of gunfire and still lost the election by six points. Id never been sure where I stood with Jim. He didnt like me, but he didnt dislike me either, as far as I could tell.
